reason: Name field is the NCT itself ('NCT02773849') — generic identifier as trial name; underlying trial (Nadofaragene firadenovec rAd-IFN/Syn3) is real (Boorjian 2021 Lancet Oncol).
PMID 33301744 resolves to a urology paper on Extended Primary Anastomosis Penile Plication (EPAPP) urethroplasty technique — completely unrelated to VISTA/oportuzumab NMIBC trial.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; VISTA real PMID is 34233186 (Dickstein 2021 J Urol)

reason: TRIMM-2 (talquetamab + daratumumab) is registered as NCT04108195, not NCT04557100; the stated NCT is one digit off from MajesTEC-1's NCT04557099 and appears fabricated by adjacency.
RedirecTT-1 (teclistamab + talquetamab) is NCT04586426, not NCT04634553; stated NCT is adjacent to MonumenTAL-1 (NCT04634552) — adjacency-fabrication pattern.
KEYNOTE-585 is registered as NCT03221426, not NCT04210115; stated NCT does not resolve to KN-585.
JCOG-1109 NExT trial is registered in UMIN-CTR (UMIN000004574), not CT.gov; NCT04007978 does not correspond to JCOG-1109.

No published 'NEoSCOPE-IO' trial exists; NEoSCOPE is a UK neoadjuvant chemoRT trial (Mukherjee 2017) without an IO arm. NCT04209115 is one digit off from the falsely-attributed NCT04210115 — adjacency-fabrication.
MYR-204 (bulevirtide + pegIFN-α) is registered as NCT03852433, not NCT04535544.
LIMT-2 (pegIFN-lambda HDV) is registered as NCT05070364, not NCT04129554; LIMT-1 was NCT02765802.
No published 'BLV-RW-EU' real-world EU 1y bulevirtide trial exists by that acronym; name follows a generic 'drug-real-world-region' pattern; NCT cannot be verified to a registered RCT.
PMID 31948863 resolves to a review on immunosuppression strategies in elderly renal transplant recipients — completely unrelated to TRANSFORM-3 esketamine elderly TRD.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; TRANSFORM-3 real PMID is 32264597 (Ochs-Ross 2020 Am J Geriatr Psychiatry)

reason: Plamotamab (XmAb13676) ph1 is NCT02924402; ALPHA-2 is the ALLO-501A allogeneic CAR-T trial (NCT04416984), not a plamotamab CD20×CD3 trial. Name/drug/NCT triple-mismatch.
ground_truth_hint: Plamotamab = NCT02924402; ALPHA-2 belongs to ALLO-501A in CAR-T review

reason: PMID 35671785 resolves to a heparin-induced thrombocytopenia case report in Am J Med — unrelated to FORTIFY risankizumab CD maintenance.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; FORTIFY real PMID 35644145 (Ferrante 2022 Lancet)

reason: PMID 30523697 resolves to a testicular germ cell tumors molecular characterization paper — unrelated to JULIET tisa-cel.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; JULIET real PMID 30501490 (Schuster 2019 NEJM)

reason: PMID 31135415 resolves to 'NewsCAP: UK journals to ban ads for infant formula' Am J Nursing news brief — unrelated to KLASS-02 laparoscopic gastrectomy trial.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; KLASS-02 real PMID 30932736 (Hyung 2020 JAMA Surg) / 31188082 (Lee 2019)

reason: LOGICA (NL laparoscopic gastrectomy) is NCT02248519, not NCT00263380; PMID 33616431 does not resolve to a real PubMed article.
ground_truth_hint: LOGICA = NCT02248519; real PMID 32985653 (van der Veen 2021 J Clin Oncol)

reason: FUGES-001 (Chinese robotic vs lap gastrectomy) is NCT03524365, not NCT05031286.

NCT01566695 is the rigosertib (Onconova) trial, not oral azacitidine. CC-486 oral azacitidine lower-risk MDS is AZA-MDS-003 (NCT01566695 is rigosertib INSPIRE/ONTIME). Name 'QUAZAR-LR-MDS' is non-standard (QUAZAR was AML-001 maintenance).
ground_truth_hint: CC-486 lower-risk MDS = NCT01566695 may belong to a different drug; QUAZAR is AML maintenance NCT01757535

reason: NCT03306264 is ASCERTAIN (decitabine-cedazuridine PO MDS, Garcia-Manero 2020 Blood) — the drug match is correct, but the acronym 'AGAVE' is not the published name of this trial.
RATIONALE-305 is NCT03777657 — correct attribution; flagged only because year=2024 PMID empty is consistent with not-yet-fully published (Qiu 2024 BMJ). No fabrication.
PMID 32145772 resolves to a Lancet COVID-19 editorial ('too little, too late?') — completely unrelated to Passeo-18 Lux DCB pivotal trial. Also Passeo-LUX trial NCT cannot be verified to NCT02862041.
PMID 24716689 resolves to a NEJM letter on solanezumab/bapineuzumab Alzheimer phase 3 trials — unrelated to KODIAC-04 naloxegol OIC.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; KODIAC-04/05 real PMID 24896818 (Chey 2014 NEJM)

reason: PMID 24716689 (same as 3091) is an unrelated Alzheimer letter — reused incorrectly for KODIAC-05.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; KODIAC-04/05 real PMID 24896818 (Chey 2014 NEJM)

reason: KODIAC-07 (naloxegol long-term safety) is NCT01395524 per published record; NCT01473960 does not match.

No published 'KODIAC-08 cancer-specific' naloxegol trial exists; the KODIAC program includes 04/05/07/08 in naloxegol filings but the cancer-OIC trial published is NCT02384538 (KODIAC was for non-cancer OIC). NCT01443403 not verifiable.
PMID 30086124 resolves to a Physician Assistant education paper on learning object repositories — unrelated to COMPOSE-3 naldemedine extension.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; COMPOSE-3 real PMID 28427293 (Webster 2017 Pain)

reason: Name 'OIC-NEW' is generic placeholder; underlying methylnaltrexone PO 450mg trial may be real (Rauck 2017 J Pain Res ~NCT01186770/NCT01186640 era) but NCT01919476 attribution is uncertain.
ground_truth_hint: rename OIC-NEW -> MNTX-MD-12 or verify NCT

reason: Name 'LUBIPROSTONE-OIC' is generic drug+indication placeholder, not a published acronym; underlying lubiprostone OIC trial (Cryer 2014) is real but should bear a study identifier (e.g. SC0931104).

Dupilumab 6-11y AD pivotal (LIBERTY AD PEDS) is NCT03345914 (registered as a single adolescent+children program); NCT03346434 is the dupilumab 6m-5y trial (LIBERTY AD PRE-SCHOOL). Name/NCT swap with row 3102.
ground_truth_hint: swap: LIBERTY-AD-PEDS-2 (6-11y) belongs to a different NCT; NCT03346434 is 6m-5y PRE-SCHOOL

reason: No published 'VOYAGE-AD' dupilumab 6m-5y trial exists by that acronym (the real 6m-5y trial is LIBERTY AD PRE-SCHOOL NCT03346434, Paller 2022 Lancet). 'VOYAGE' is a guselkumab psoriasis trial name. NCT04394741 not verifiable to dupilumab.
ground_truth_hint: likely fabricated; real 6m-5y is LIBERTY AD PRE-SCHOOL NCT03346434

reason: JADE TEEN (abrocitinib adolescent) is NCT03796676, not NCT04345367; 'JADE-PED' is non-standard.

Upadacitinib adolescent AD was studied within Measure Up 1/2 (NCT03569293/NCT03607422), not as a separate 'Measure-UP-PED' (NCT04195814). The 'PED' suffix and NCT do not appear in the published literature.
ground_truth_hint: likely fabricated; adolescent data come from Measure Up 1/2 subgroups

reason: PMID 32074416 resolves to NEJM perspective on COVID-19 epidemiology — unrelated to Elaris UF-1 elagolix fibroids HMB trial.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; Elaris UF-1/UF-2 real PMID 31995689 (Schlaff 2020 NEJM)

reason: Same PMID 32074416 reused — COVID-19 NEJM perspective, not Elaris UF-2.
ground_truth_hint: null PMID; Elaris UF-1/UF-2 real PMID 31995689 (Schlaff 2020 NEJM)

reason: SPIRIT-1 NCT03204318 is genuine (relugolix endometriosis SPIRIT program); same NCT also used (correctly) in the ENDOMETRIOSIS review row 3057. Cross-review duplication is expected, no fabrication.

NCT01931670 is ELARIS-EM-2 (per NEJM Taylor 2017), not 'Elaris EM-1'. Row 3055 in the ENDOMETRIOSIS review correctly assigns NCT01620528 to ELARIS-EM-1 and NCT01931670 to ELARIS-EM-2. This row swaps the EM-1/EM-2 NCTs.
NCT01760954 is the elagolix long-term endometriosis safety/efficacy EXTENSION study, not 'Elaris EM-2'. ELARIS-EM-2 is NCT01931670. NCT-to-acronym misattribution.
ground_truth_hint: Elaris EM-2 = NCT01931670; NCT01760954 is the EXTENSION

reason: No published 'EDELWEISS-6' linzagolix ph3 trial exists; EDELWEISS-3 (NCT03654274, Donnez 2022) is the only published endometriosis ph3 in that program. EDELWEISS-6 acronym + 2024 year + NCT03992846 collectively unverifiable.
PRIMROSE-1 (linzagolix uterine fibroids) is NCT03070899, not NCT04244175. Row 3113 in the GnRH review correctly cites NCT03070899 for PRIMROSE-1.
PRIMROSE-2 is NCT03070951, not NCT04244162. Row 3114 in the GnRH review correctly cites NCT03070951.
Name 'GO29781' is the Roche sponsor protocol code, not the published acronym (the trial is published as Budde 2022 Lancet Oncol mosunetuzumab pivotal). Functional but not an acronym.
Name 'NP30179' is the Roche sponsor protocol code for glofitamab DLBCL ph1/2 (Hutchings 2021, Dickinson 2022 NEJM). Functional but not the published acronym.
